stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta's Llama 3 8B model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key ML/NLP tags including 'transformers', 'llama', and 'text-generation'.
This indicates active community interest and benchmarking of a significant, modern LLM release.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It pertains to the core field of large language models (LLMs) and text generation.
The associated tags indicate relevance to transformers, safetensors, and the Llama family of models.

stories.title: Google won’t just admit it’s feeding YouTube creators to its music AI
stories.author: Terrence O’Brien
bullet_points: Google is allegedly using music uploaded to YouTube to train its Lyria music AI without explicit consent.
A group of independent musicians has filed a lawsuit against Google over the alleged illegal use of their copyrighted material.
This case highlights growing legal and ethical concerns regarding the use of user-generated content in training large-scale generative AI models.

stories.title: Nobody needs AI to search the Internet, court says in ruling against Google
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: A German court ruled against Google regarding its AI search features.
The ruling suggests that AI is not a necessary component for internet searching.
The outcome could significantly impact the trajectory of the AI search industry.

stories.title: ABC-Bench: An Agentic Bio-Capabilities Benchmark for Biosecurity
stories.author: Seth Donoughe
bullet_points: LLMs are gaining significant capabilities in biological research, including data interpretation and in silico biology tasks.
The rise of these AI capabilities presents both opportunities for scientific discovery and new biosecurity risks.
The paper introduces ABC-Bench, a benchmark specifically designed to evaluate agentic bio-capabilities in LLMs.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text generation and conversational AI tasks.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular tool for running local LLMs, has integrated MLX.
This integration significantly boosts performance specifically on Apple Silicon hardware.
The feature is currently available in a preview release.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, ensuring data processing occurs entirely on the user's device.
The core value proposition is providing private, on-device AI capabilities for coding assistance.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to simplify the setup and execution of coding tools.
It allows users to run tools like Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex using local or cloud models.
A key feature is the zero-configuration requirement, eliminating the need for environment variables or config files.

stories.title: Microsoft restricts Claude Fable for employees over data retention concerns
stories.author: Tom Warren
bullet_points: Microsoft is restricting internal employee access to Anthropic's new Claude Fable model.
The restriction stems from Anthropic's new data retention requirements.
The model has already been rolled out to external customers like GitHub Copilot and Foundry.

stories.title: Google will save your Lens photos, Search Live recordings, and Translate audio for AI training
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: Google is expanding data collection to include user interactions with AI-powered features like Google Lens and Search Live.
User data, including images, audio, and video used in searches, will be saved under a new 'Search Services History' setting.
This change indicates a significant shift in how Google leverages user data for training its artificial intelligence models.

stories.title: Google's latest DiffusionGemma open AI model comes with a 4x speed boost
stories.author: Ryan Whitwam
bullet_points: Google has released DiffusionGemma, an open AI model.
The new model features a significant 4x speed improvement.
The technology demonstrates that Diffusion AI can accelerate text generation, not just image creation.

stories.title: Data Journalist Agent: Transforming Data into Verifiable Multimodal Stories
stories.author: James Zou
bullet_points: Introduces a 'Data Journalist Agent' aiming to automate the end-to-end process of transforming raw data into trustworthy, multimodal news stories.
Addresses the current limitation where existing AI agents handle only isolated steps (e.g., data analysis or visual design) rather than the holistic journalistic process.
Highlights the challenge of replicating the complex, multi-stage workflow of a human data journalist, which typically takes weeks.

stories.title: The Shibboleth Effect: Auditing the Cross-Lingual Distributional Skew of Large Language Models
stories.author: Hakan Mehmetcik
bullet_points: Investigates the 'Shibboleth Effect,' a cross-lingual distributional skew in frontier LLMs.
Uses a complex, synthetic geopolitical wargame ('Cerulean Sea Crisis') to test models under adversarial conditions.
Evaluates six leading LLMs (including GPT-4o, Gemini-3.1-Pro, etc.) for biases related to language and geopolitical scenarios.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The pipeline being showcased is 'text-generation', indicating a focus on generative AI.
The associated tags (transformers, pytorch, safetensors) confirm its relevance within the modern NLP/ML ecosystem.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release brings improved performance and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across various hardware.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine, particularly benefiting Apple silicon users.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA has announced the Nemotron 3 Ultra model.
The model is specifically designed for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is optimized for supporting long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, providing an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The key feature is that these agents can run locally on the user's own hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, a popular local LLM runner.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports local image generation on macOS.
This feature allows users to run image generation models offline on their local machines.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to run tools like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source local models and proprietary API capabilities.

stories.title: Claude Fable won’t answer basic biology questions
stories.author: Robert Hart
bullet_points: Anthropic released Claude Fable 5, claiming it is their most powerful widely available AI model.
Despite marketing claims, the new model failed to answer basic biology questions.
The model instead redirected simple queries to a different system or resource.

stories.title: You can just tell the Instagram algorithm what you want now
stories.author: Jay Peters
bullet_points: Instagram is introducing a 'Your Algorithm' feature allowing users to influence content shown in their main feed.
The feature enables users to view and modify the topics Instagram believes they are interested in across the platform.
This represents a shift toward giving users more control over algorithmic curation on a major social media platform.

stories.title: Racist comments targeting politicians tripled since Meta relaxed its rules
stories.author: David Gilbert, wired.com
bullet_points: Racist comments targeting politicians have reportedly tripled since Meta adjusted its content moderation policies.
The surge in harmful content is linked to Meta relaxing its rules on the platform.
Violent threats directed at lawmakers have also shown a significant increase on Facebook.

stories.title: GM Energy introduces V2G support and new energy storage battery chemistry
stories.author: Roberto Baldwin
bullet_points: GM Energy is expanding its EV ecosystem by introducing Vehicle-to-Grid (V2G) support.
The announcement highlights the growing adoption of V2G-capable GM EVs, with over 250,000 units already on the road.
GM is also advancing its energy storage capabilities through the introduction of new battery chemistry.

stories.title: Starlink charges $10 monthly hardware fee in move away from one-time purchases
stories.author: Jon Brodkin
bullet_points: Starlink is implementing a new pricing structure, shifting from one-time hardware purchases to a monthly hardware fee.
The service subscription price has also seen an increase, rising by $5 to a new rate of $10.
This change impacts Starlink, which is noted as SpaceX's primary revenue generator.

stories.title: EEVEE: Towards Test-time Prompt Learning in the Real World for Self-Improving Agents
stories.author: Mengdi Wang
bullet_points: Introduces EEVEE, a novel framework for test-time prompt learning in LLM agents.
Addresses a key limitation in current methods by supporting multi-dataset, real-world task streams instead of single-dataset settings.
Employs a router mechanism to mitigate cross-dataset interference during learning.

stories.title: Piper: A Programmable Distributed Training System
stories.author: Stephanie Wang
bullet_points: The paper introduces 'Piper,' a programmable distributed training system designed to manage complex parallelism strategies.
Current large-scale model training requires combining multiple parallelism techniques (data, pipeline, expert) with memory optimizations (e.g., ZeRO).
Piper aims to automate the manual, expert-driven design of high-level parallelism strategies, improving system adaptability.

stories.title: Flaws in the LLM Automation Narrative
stories.author: Marc Scott
bullet_points: The narrative that LLMs perform at human expert level on knowledge tasks is being questioned.
Current benchmarks often measure performance on content already present in the LLM's training data.
A key limitation of existing evaluations is the failure to assess the reliability or magnitude of LLM performance.

stories.title: ReasonAlloc: Hierarchical Decoding-Time KV Cache Budget Allocation for Reasoning Models
stories.author: Shuang Qiu
bullet_points: ReasonAlloc addresses inference bottlenecks in LLM reasoning caused by rapid Key-Value (KV) cache growth during long Chain-of-Thought (CoT) trajectories.
It introduces a novel hierarchical decoding-time KV cache budget allocation mechanism.
The method improves upon existing techniques by moving beyond uniform budget distribution and addressing the stepwise context demands during decoding.

stories.title: When to Align, When to Predict: A Phase Diagram for Multimodal Learning
stories.author: Randall Balestriero
bullet_points: The paper addresses the lack of systematic understanding regarding when to use Cross-modal Alignment (CA) versus Cross-modal Prediction (CP) in multimodal learning.
It proposes a 'Phase Diagram' to help practitioners determine the optimal training paradigm for different multimodal tasks.
The research is highly relevant to scientific domains (e.g., biomedicine, astrophysics) where heterogeneous data and complex measurements are common.

stories.title: A Unifying Lens on Supervised Fine-Tuning Through Target Distribution Design
stories.author: Cho-Jui Hsieh
bullet_points: The paper reinterprets Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) not just as maximizing token likelihood, but as a problem of target distribution design.
It argues that strictly fitting to observed tokens can be suboptimal due to noise, non-uniqueness, or misalignment with the model's prior knowledge.
The work analyzes the token-level target distribution that the loss function implicitly drives during SFT.

stories.title: EEVEE: Towards Test-time Prompt Learning in the Real World for Self-Improving Agents
stories.author: Mengdi Wang
bullet_points: Introduces EEVEE, a novel framework for test-time prompt learning in LLM agents.
Addresses a critical limitation in current methods by supporting multi-dataset, real-world task streams.
Employs a router mechanism to mitigate cross-dataset interference during learning.

stories.title: Predicting Future Behaviors in Reasoning Models Enables Better Steering
stories.author: Wojciech Samek
bullet_points: The paper addresses the issue of unpredictable behavior in large reasoning models (LRMs) during deployment.
Existing 'test-time steering' methods, which intervene on hidden representations, can degrade output quality.
The research proposes training activation probes to predict future behavioral outcomes, suggesting a better intervention target than current detection features.

stories.title: COGENT: Continuous Graph Emulators with Neural Ordinary Differential Equations for Long-Term Physical Forecasting
stories.author: Maryam Rahnemoonfar
bullet_points: Introduces COGENT, a continuous graph emulator designed for long-term physical forecasting.
Utilizes Neural Ordinary Differential Equations (NODEs) to model temporal evolution on irregular geospatial meshes.
Employs a graph-based history encoder to capture both local spatial interactions and temporal system states.

stories.title: Itô maps for any-step SDEs
stories.author: Jakiw Pidstrigach
bullet_points: Introduces the 'Itô map,' a novel stochastic flow map designed to predict future states from intermediate states and Brownian paths in a single pass.
Addresses a limitation in current generative models that rely on ODEs, providing a method for handling stochastic dynamics (SDEs).
The formulation offers new estimators relevant for inference-time control applications in stochastic systems.

stories.title: OncoTraj: a public benchmark for longitudinal resistance prediction in EGFR-mutant non-small-cell lung cancer on osimertinib
stories.author: Aarchi Singh Thakur
bullet_points: Introduces OncoTraj, a new public benchmark dataset for studying resistance prediction in EGFR-mutant NSCLC.
The dataset comprises longitudinal patient trajectories from 813 patients treated with osimertinib.
Addresses a critical gap in computational oncology by providing a standardized resource for training and evaluating models on clonal evolution.

stories.title: PhantomBench: Benchmarking the Non-existential Threat of Language Models
stories.author: Hila Gonen
bullet_points: Introduces PhantomBench, a new large-scale benchmark specifically designed to test language model hallucinations.
Addresses the critical issue of LLM hallucinations, where models generate factually incorrect or ungrounded information.
Highlights the risk of over-reliance on LMs, especially in high-stakes domains where errors can cause significant harm.

stories.title: Modeling Complex Behaviors: Multi-Personality Composition and Dynamic Switching in Vision-Language Models
stories.author: Zhou Su
bullet_points: Addresses the need to control complex behaviors in Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) for social interaction.
Proposes a method for explicit personality conditioning and a systematic evaluation framework (single, multi, and switching personalities).
Findings indicate that while personality induction boosts image captioning, it can negatively impact performance.
